title: Math teacher chat — phased build
description: Private math-lesson instructor on the client chat surface (/client/<brand> in Kody-Dashboard): guide students through lessons stored in the data layer. DECIDED SEQUENCE (owner, 2026-07-09): build phases 1-3 to genuinely working on main, put a real student through them, and let that experience shape 4-7. Do NOT build all phases up front. All work happens in the Kody-Dashboard repo (aharonyaircohen/Kody-Dashboard, main branch, no feature branches); this repo (A-Guy-Teacher) is the consumer: its state holds brand files, lessons, and student records. Relevant existing pieces: client auth (src/dashboard/lib/client-auth/, brand auth block in brands/<slug>.json), CMS entity API (/api/kody/cms/<collection>), view renderers (/views/renderers), setup wizards (/setup).

items.title: Phase 1 — Talking teacher
items.body: Zero dashboard code. In this repo's state: create agents/math-teacher.md (persona: patient private math teacher; persona = identity/values only, no task lists), write one full lesson (explanation, examples, exercises with answers, pass criteria) into the brand's welcome/instructions path, and set agentSlug: math-teacher plus auth block on brands/<slug>.json. Verify: student signs in with Google, chat teaches the lesson, quizzes, corrects. Known gap fixed 2026-07-09: client-surface chat turns now set repo context (kody route) — retest after changes.

items.title: Phase 2 — Lessons from storage
items.body: Lessons become data. Define a 'lessons' content model (CMS, /content pages; fields: slug, title, level, body-md, exercises, passRule) stored via the storage adapter in this repo's state. Add a chat tool on the kody-direct route (app/api/kody/chat/kody — see existing tools in app/api/kody/chat/tools/) that lists lessons and fetches one by slug, allowed on the client surface. The agent prompt then says: fetch the lesson the student picks and teach from it verbatim-structure, own words.

items.title: Phase 3 — Student memory
items.body: THE DATA DOOR — the real build. New API path that accepts the client session cookie (Auth.js `auth()` from src/dashboard/lib/client-auth/auth.ts) instead of admin headers, resolves the student's verified email, and reads/writes records in a 'progress' collection ALWAYS scoped server-side by that email (stamp on write, filter on read; client can never pick the scope). Expose as chat tools (get_my_progress / save_my_progress) so the agent resumes where the student left off. Records: {email, lessonSlug, position, score, updatedAt}.

items.title: Phase 4 — Structured lessons
items.body: Lesson files gain structure the AI must follow: ordered steps (say / ask+expected-answer / exercise), pass rules, branching ('struggling -> remediation step'), per-step guardrails (never reveal quiz answers, no skipping), resume via phase-3 position, an outcome record on completion (score, completedAt), and a version field on lesson + run state (no full version management yet — just the field). Mirror the setup-wizard engine pattern: declarative steps validated by zod, the AI is the runner (chat), not buttons.

items.title: Phase 5 — Visual pages from JSON primitives
items.body: Pages are JSON compositions of the EXISTING view-renderer primitives (src/dashboard/lib/view-renderers/definition.ts: stack/row/list/text/input/actions) — decided: NO free HTML, NO new page language. Render a progress board / quiz panel beside the client chat (panel slot in the chat platform contract). Interactions go through one bridge (kody.call style) hitting the phase-3 API; permissions stay server-side.

items.title: Phase 6 — Authoring by chat
items.body: Teacher (admin) creates/edits lessons by chatting: engine or kody-direct tool writes lesson entities + page JSON, schema-validated before save so a bad generation fails validation instead of corrupting state. Every authored artifact is a normal git-versioned state file (reviewable diff).

items.title: Phase 7 — Brand component libraries (escape hatch)
items.body: Escape hatch — build ONLY when a real brand outgrows the platform primitives. A business ships its own component library (custom-elements JS/CSS in its repo, any design complexity); page JSON places brand components next to platform primitives. Markup is free but data/actions still flow only through the platform API with the user's identity — every permission enforced server-side, never in the page.
